summary: gpt-sdk postinstall exfiltrates host, Active Directory, and CI metadata to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script (postinstall.js) collects installer-side identifiers — hostname, reverse-DNS FQDN, Active Directory domain (USERDNSDOMAIN), user domain, logon server, proxy presence, install working directory and full path, the configured npm registry URL, and CI repository slugs from GitHub Actions, CircleCI, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps, Travis, and Jenkins — and sends them via plain HTTP GET to http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping. The destination is a hardcoded bare IP with no TLS and no opt-out, directly contradicting the README's claim that 'No data is collected'. The package name 'gpt-sdk' impersonates OpenAI's GPT SDK naming to attract mistaken installs, and the author's own description characterizes the package as a 'honeypot' — i.e., it relies on accidental installation to deliver the reconnaissance beacon. The harvested fields (corporate AD domain, internal hostnames, internal npm registry, repo identifiers) are valuable reconnaissance for follow-on targeting of the installer's environment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
